title: myriopus parvifolius
intro: Myriopus parvifolius, also known as sticky gilia or sticky gilia seed, is a plant species belonging to the genus Myriopus, which is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Myriopus parvifolius include its ability to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves and seeds, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
